explanation: The requirement states that when the system is in Start-up Mode (StartUpMode), the default parameters must be loaded from a configuration file (defaultParamsLoaded). This is best expressed as: 'Whenever StartUpMode holds, defaultParamsLoaded must eventually hold within a certain duration.' Therefore, we globally specify (using the LTL template G (bool_exp1 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))) with bool_exp1 as StartUpMode and bool_exp3 as defaultParamsLoaded, and N_DURATION set to 10. All boolean expression placeholders are valid and non-empty.

The natural language requirement states that, upon start-up (i.e., when the machine is turned on and in StartUpMode), the system shall load a set of default parameter values from a configuration file. The atomic proposition 'defaultParamsLoaded' indicates that the default parameters have actually been loaded, and 'StartUpMode' indicates the system is in the start-up mode. To capture that, whenever the system is in StartUpMode, it must be the case that defaultParamsLoaded becomes true (either immediately or after some bounded initialization steps). The chosen template is "G (bool_exp1 -> F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3)", which expresses that globally, whenever StartUpMode holds, defaultParamsLoaded must eventually (within N_DURATION steps) become true. This directly models the requirement that default values must be loaded during start-up. N_DURATION is chosen as 5 (arbitrary, can be adjusted to system context).

The property requires that whenever the system enters Start-up Mode (StartUpMode), it always follows that, within N_DURATION steps, the default parameters are loaded (defaultParamsLoaded). This is direct from the requirement: on power-up/start-up, defaults should be loaded. The template is 'G (bool_exp1 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))', which ensures, every time StartUpMode is true, defaultParamsLoaded must eventually hold within N_DURATION. Here, bool_exp1 is StartUpMode and bool_exp3 is defaultParamsLoaded. bool_exp2 is set to 'true' as a vacuous / neutral value since this template does not use it.

The requirement states that default parameter values must be loaded from a configuration file when the machine is turned on, i.e., at Start-up Mode. In the atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' indicates the start-up mode is active, and 'defaultParamsLoaded' indicates the default parameters have been loaded. The LTL property should express that whenever the system enters Start-up Mode, then (eventually) the default parameters are loaded. This is best captured using the template: G (StartUpMode -> F[0,N_DURATION] defaultParamsLoaded), ensuring that on every activation of Start-up Mode, loading of default parameters will occur within bounded time N_DURATION. This corresponds to template 14. N_DURATION is set to a reasonable value (e.g., 10).

The input requirement specifies that when the system is in Start-up Mode, default parameter values must be provided and loaded from a configuration file. From the atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' captures when the machine is in Start-up Mode, and 'defaultParamsLoaded' indicates the event of default parameters being loaded. Therefore, the LTL property should express that whenever Start-up Mode is true, within a certain bounded time (e.g., 10 steps), default parameters must eventually be loaded (defaultParamsLoaded becomes true). This matches the template: G (bool_exp1 -> F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3), with bool_exp1 = StartUpMode, bool_exp3 = defaultParamsLoaded, and N_DURATION = 10. No variables are needed for bool_exp2 or bool_exp4, so these are left empty strings (the template does not use bool_exp2 or bool_exp4). This property is well-suited to capture the expected start-up protocol behavior.

The requirement states that, during Start-up Mode, default values for all parameters must be loaded from a configuration file when the machine is powered on. Translating this to LTL using the available atomic propositions, we want to express that: whenever the system is in StartUpMode, the defaultParamsLoaded proposition becomes true (the default parameters are loaded from the config file). Therefore, we use an invariant implication: 'It is always the case that if StartUpMode is active, then defaultParamsLoaded is (or becomes) true.' This directly enforces the requirement that defaults are loaded whenever the system is in StartUpMode. Template 30 captures this meaning with bool_exp1='StartUpMode' and bool_exp3='defaultParamsLoaded'. N_DURATION is not required for this template.

The input says that whenever the machine is turned on (that is, leaves the 'off' state), a set of default parameter values must be loaded from the configuration file. In the provided atomic propositions, 'off' indicates that the system is completely de-energized and off, and 'defaultParamsLoaded' signals when the default parameters have been loaded. We therefore express that whenever the system turns on (i.e., when 'off' becomes false in the next moment), 'defaultParamsLoaded' must hold immediately after. This guarantees that on startup, the parameters are loaded as specified.

The natural language requirement states that when the system is powered on, a set of default values for all parameters must be loaded from a configuration file on the machine. This is an operational constraint that, when the system enters StartUpMode (which implies power-on), the loading of default parameters (defaultParamsLoaded) must occur. This is best captured as an invariant: globally, whenever StartUpMode is true (i.e., the system is in Start-up Mode), it should imply that the default parameters have been loaded (defaultParamsLoaded is true). This is achieved using template 30: 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)' with bool_exp1='StartUpMode', bool_exp3='defaultParamsLoaded'. N_DURATION is not needed for this template.

The natural language requirement states that whenever the machine is turned on, the default parameter values shall be loaded from a configuration file. From the provided atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' indicates the start-up mode (i.e., machine turned on), and 'defaultParamsLoaded' indicates that default parameter values have been loaded. Therefore, globally, whenever 'StartUpMode' holds, it should always be followed by 'defaultParamsLoaded'. Thus, the invariant 'G (StartUpMode -> defaultParamsLoaded)' precisely captures the requirement that on entering start-up mode, the default parameters must be loaded.

The natural language requirement specifies that a set of default parameters shall be provided and loaded from a configuration file when the machine turns on (i.e., at start-up). The best mapping is that, globally (at all times), when the system is in StartUpMode, the default parameters must eventually be loaded. To express the causal implication ('when in StartUpMode, defaultParamsLoaded must happen within a bounded time'), I chose template 14: 'G (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))' where bool_exp2 is StartUpMode and bool_exp3 is defaultParamsLoaded. N_DURATION is set to a reasonable value of 5, since this is a step that should complete during initialization. This formula captures that for every time the system enters Start-up Mode, within 5 steps, the default parameters are loaded.
